note: Real Singapore enrichment providers, researched. Prices are typical monthly estimates and change often — confirm with the centre.

classes.rev: Bilingual creative-drama focus on body language and imaginative roleplay for the youngest learners.
Boutique two-outlet abacus centre in Bukit Timah and Katong; small groups but among the priciest abacus options.
Process-art franchise that rotates through real artists and styles, with a gallery share at the end of each session.
Short weekly preschool sessions drilling speech through tongue twisters and role-play.
Singapore's first dedicated children's drama academy, with story-based classes for toddlers up; reliable but on the pricier side.
East-side gym offering cheap $15 trial classes, a low-risk way to test if your child takes to it.
Award-winning Chinese speech and drama that leans on crafts, songs and props to keep young kids engaged.
Award-winning chain that wraps fine-art technique in games, stories and songs; membership-based so commitment is expected.
Keeps a tight one-instructor-to-four-children ratio, so kids get genuine individual guidance.
Based at Orchid Country Club with a clear beginner-to-advanced ladder; location may be a stretch for non-northeast families.
Runs out of HomeTeamNS and SAFRA clubhouses and notably caters to special-needs gymnasts too.
A long-running, affordable recreational gym across pools and community clubs; strong on playful coordination rather than competitive polish.
Well-known Chinese enrichment with songs and storytelling across 20+ malls; very accessible.
West-side abacus specialist with a free trial and assessment; handy for Jurong/Clementi but region-limited.
Franchise model means easy access islandwide; LEGO-driven and light on actual coding.
Qualified native-standard teachers and small groups; trusted but expensive and limited locations.
A Holland Village mainstay with a clear progression from Kindergym into recreational and competitive tracks.

classes.prov: Centre Stage School of the Arts
classes.rev: Long-running studio with internationally trained teachers and an early-childhood creative-play track.
Choice-based approach lets children pick their own projects; suits self-directed kids more than those wanting structure.
Speech-and-drama approach to Mandarin from the Julia Gabriel group; immersive but premium.
Veteran centre with small classes; strong on building, less obviously on coding.
Veteran, well-priced abacus school across Toa Payoh/Bishan/Novena; no-frills and traditional.
Long-established dual-hand abacus chain with the widest island footprint; effective for speed but drill-heavy.
Award-winning and rigorous, but it skews older (7+) and academic rather than play-led.
Multi-sensory approach with rare dyslexia-intervention training; strong for struggling readers.
Official LEGO Education and KUBO partner, so the kit and curriculum are legit.
Learn-by-doing with LEGO models lands well for the 4-6 crowd; lighter on real coding.
Dual piano-and-violin early-years track, though it caps out around age five.
Wide age range and a free trial; framing leans hard on DSA portfolio prep even for little ones.
Covers both artistic and rhythmic gymnastics across community venues, though the youngest classes start at five.
Structured 10-level 9-bead abacus course across heartland outlets; solid progression but traditional and repetitive.
Five eastern/central outlets with transparent pricing and a refundable trial; classic mental-arithmetic drilling.
Long-running structured curriculum spanning painting, handicrafts and comics; methodical rather than free-spirited.
Non-profit studio offering a structured ladder from foundation to diploma levels.
Values-led acting workshops emphasising authenticity and empathy over polished performance.
Fast-paced right-brain programme with flash-based numeracy; engaging for very young kids but expensive.
Globally franchised drama curriculum pitched at life-skills and confidence rather than stage careers.
Instructors work closely with each child regardless of ability, with branches sited in malls islandwide.
Established Mandarin centre (since 1992) in four malls; foundational skills then MOE-aligned later.
Confidence-focused programme with digital tools and virtual options; very widely available.
Adaptive, proficiency-based curriculum is a plus; orientation tilts toward competitions.
Preschool-focused multi-sport classes built around fundamental movement skills before introducing specific sports.
Play-filled phonics with songs and visuals that builds a solid early reading base; only a few outlets.
Small-group taster of singing, keyboard, percussion, ukulele and violin before committing to one instrument.
Premium EduDrama methodology with strong communication outcomes, but among the most expensive options.
Introduces both piano and violin to 4-6s, a solid way to test which instrument actually sticks.
Globally recognised play-based music-and-movement programme with several mall studios and a virtual option.
Project-based with a real-world bent; young-age fit is plausible but not clearly stated.
Self-paced worksheet method building vocabulary and recall; effective but repetitive, not play-based.
Scaffolded three-stage reading path across many outlets; methodical rather than flashy.
A short two-day workshop format for a confidence boost rather than ongoing weekly training.
Seven-level reading ladder from letters to multisyllabic words; clear progression, niche centre.
Award-winning East-Coast studio that also accommodates children with learning differences; fairly far east.
Long-running (since 1993) phonics centre with experienced teachers; small and old-school.
Long-established centre with strong teacher quality and multisensory lessons; premium positioning.
Well-established communicator-focused programmes spanning preschool to advanced levels.
Confidence-building blend of speech, drama and EQ aimed at nursery to K2.
A robot companion and progress-tracking app make it feel modern for early learners.
Multi-discipline arts programme combining drama with music and dance under experienced directors.
Game-based Vedic-math approach that keeps young kids engaged through play; fun, though shortcuts diverge from the MOE syllabus.
Three north-east/central outlets with mid-range fees and a small trial; reliable drilling without frills.
Play-based early-concept maths aimed at preschoolers; gentle and exploratory, thin public detail.
Tiny classes (max 6, two staff) and trilingual support; single East Coast location limits access.
Takes kids as young as 3 with a hands-on path up to AI; good fit for the youngest.
Systematic 42-sound phonics with structured book progression; solid traditional method.
Tuition-chain phonics with proprietary books across many outlets; leans academic.
Premium brand pairing heuristics with a critical-thinking pitch; polished but priced high.
Big chain with a structured, assessment-first literacy curriculum; reliable but corporate and pricey.
Single-location, MOE-aligned hands-on maths at SAFRA Toa Payoh; convenient there but limited reach.
Interactive etiquette and resilience lessons focused on social skills more than performance.
Combines music, dance and drama for preschoolers; good for active kids who learn best by moving.
Joo Chiat studio catering to ages 3-12 with a warm, kid-focused vibe popular with eastside parents.
Works from the belief that art is a learnable skill, with a relaxed Hougang space that doubles for parties.
Five-element literacy curriculum with classes capped at 10; free assessment helps level matching.
A well-known franchise with four outlets and structured age-banded classes; great energy but franchise pricing adds up.
Arts-school-grade bilingual drama blending artistic growth with language development.
Long-running robotics specialist; the youngest tier starts around 6, so the 4-5 set is out.
Tanglin studio with age-banded classes from babies to age seven, blending singing, instruments and movement.
Solid mall-based recreational gym with holiday camps and parties; pricing isn't published so you'll need to enquire.
Toa Payoh club leaning toward artistic gymnastics; the per-term fee is on the higher side for casual learners.
Structured world-champion-led method with a cheap trial, though core age skews older than 8.
In-person and cheaper online abacus classes with a free session; flexible, but skews age 6 and up.
Pasir Ris studio that pairs art-making with confidence and problem-solving; handy for far-east families.
Bukit Timah academy with both recreational and serious competitive pathways, suiting kids who may want to progress.
Fast, results-driven course promising 500 words in six sessions; bold claim, intensive pacing.
A physio-designed, non-competitive programme teaching ten sports across 50+ preschools, ideal for building broad movement basics under age six.
One of the few centres focused on rhythmic gymnastics, blending dance-like artistry with athletic discipline.
MOE-aligned progression is a selling point but the youngest classes really begin at 5.
Genuinely exploratory and creativity-first, though one main campus limits convenience.
Hands-on exhibits are excellent for young kids but it's drop-in workshops, not a steady weekly class.
Award-winning bilingual centre focused on confidence and expressive performance.
Self-paced worksheet-based Singapore Math with strong heuristics and problem-solving; broad coverage, though the self-directed format suits self-motivated kids more.
Premium right-brain method weaving in number and memory work; strong on early stimulation but pricey.
Vocals-and-movement musical-theatre track culminating in end-of-year public performances.
Mediacorp-backed programme using storytelling and drama for speech skills, but term fees run high.
Heartland-focused abacus chain with a free first session; affordable and steady, purely arithmetic.
Observe-question-experiment approach built into a preschool, so it's gentle and age-right.
Pairs phonics with public-speaking confidence; good for shy readers, less for pure decoding.
Story-driven 'speak up and stand tall' approach with a clear emotional-intelligence emphasis.
Broad STEM menu including VR; preschool coverage is real but heartland-only locations.
Trinity College London partner offering drama in English, Hindi and Mandarin.
Competition-pedigree centre; no published ages so younger fit is unconfirmed.
Small-group classes built around independence and reflection, drawing out each child's own personality.
Inquiry-based experiments suit curious preschoolers, with preschool and lower-primary tracks.
Curiosity-driven phonics with songs and parent feedback; warm and child-led.
Playground-style setup with a clear block-to-Python path and competition results to back it.
Rigorous 'encode, retrieve, apply' literacy from a high-end tuition brand; academically intense.
Polished Orchard-based programme with a clear curriculum; premium positioning means it's pricier than community-club options.
Lays a structured musical foundation for under-fives, aimed at families serious about eventual instrument study.
Bilingual English/Mandarin programme built around imagination, empathy and language fluency.
Multiple outlets combining gymnastics, parkour and trampoline; a good fit for kids who like to climb and tumble freely.
Specialist Mandarin school with 11 islandwide outlets; structured and exam-oriented as kids grow.
Performing-arts provider with a gentle StoryPlay-to-speech progression from infancy up.
Imaginative studio covering painting, sculpture and ceramics, so kids get genuine variety.
Tampines Hub gym with discounted trial classes; the term commitment of 11 lessons plus a registration fee is worth checking before signing.
Globally recognised abacus franchise with 15+ centres and a free assessment; quality can vary by branch.
Play-based multi-sport sessions emphasising character and fun over competition, suited to younger or hesitant kids.
Korean-curriculum-inspired classes with an age-banded Little Explorers tier; single Orchard location.
Theatre-company-run classes that empower kids to experiment and make creative decisions.
Deep phonics paired with genuine literary appreciation; more reading-for-love than drill.
Invention-led blend of STEM and making; single hotel-based location is the main catch.
Playful kinesthetic method using animal-shaped letters; great for active young learners.
